source: W3C DID Use Cases and Requirements (NOTE-did-use-cases-20210317)

requirements.name: Authentication / Proof of Control
requirements.description: The DID method MUST support cryptographic authentication and proof of control over the identifier.

requirements.name: Decentralized / Self-Issued
requirements.description: Identifiers MUST be created without a central registration authority.

requirements.name: Guaranteed Unique Identifier
requirements.description: The method MUST guarantee global uniqueness of identifiers.

requirements.name: No Call Home
requirements.description: Resolution MUST NOT require contacting the issuer or any single third party.

requirements.name: Associated Cryptographic Material
requirements.description: The DID Document MUST include public keys or verification methods for cryptographic operations.

requirements.name: Streamlined Key Rotation
requirements.description: The method MUST support key rotation without changing the DID identifier.

requirements.name: Service Endpoint Discovery
requirements.description: The DID Document MUST support service endpoints for messaging, credential exchange, and other interactions.

requirements.name: Privacy Preserving
requirements.description: The method MUST minimize personally identifiable information stored on-chain or in the DID Document.

requirements.name: Delegation of Control
requirements.description: The method MUST support delegating control over a DID to other DIDs or keys.

requirements.name: Inter-Jurisdictional
requirements.description: The method MUST work across legal jurisdictions without requiring jurisdiction-specific infrastructure.

requirements.name: Cannot Be Administratively Denied
requirements.description: No single administrative authority can revoke or deny a DID without the controller's consent.

requirements.name: Minimized Rents
requirements.description: Ongoing costs for maintaining a DID SHOULD be minimal or zero.

requirements.name: No Vendor Lock-In
requirements.description: The DID and its associated data MUST be portable across implementations and providers.

requirements.name: Preempt / Limit Trackable Data Trails
requirements.description: The method SHOULD minimize correlation across interactions and contexts.

requirements.name: Cryptographic Future-Proof
requirements.description: The method SHOULD define a migration path for post-quantum cryptography or algorithm agility.

requirements.name: Survives Issuing Organization Mortality
requirements.description: The DID MUST remain resolvable and usable even if the original issuing organization ceases to exist.

requirements.name: Survives Deployment End-of-Life
requirements.description: The DID MUST remain usable if a specific software deployment is retired.

requirements.name: Survives Relationship with Service Provider
requirements.description: The DID MUST remain under the controller's authority regardless of their relationship with any service provider.

requirements.name: Cryptographic Authentication and Communication
requirements.description: The method MUST enable authenticated, encrypted communication between DID controllers.

requirements.name: Registry Agnostic
requirements.description: The method SHOULD NOT depend on a single verifiable data registry.

requirements.name: Legally-Enabled Identity
requirements.description: The method SHOULD support binding a DID to a legal identity where required by regulation.

requirements.name: Human-Centered Interoperability
requirements.description: The method SHOULD support human-readable identifiers or aliases for usability.
